summary: Good audience recording of a solid August ’82 show, highlighted by Althea, China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider and a fiery Playin’/Morning Dew sequence.
review: The second‑set‑only audience tape captures an energetic late‑summer 1982 performance with strong interplay between Jerry and Brent, clear Phil bass, and a standout flow that moves from Althea through China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider into a scorching Playin’/Space/Playin’ Reprise leading into Morning Dew.  While the mix is generally good – especially the second‑set levels – some listeners note noise‑reduction artifacts and low‑end distortion that keep it from being pristine.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality is consistently praised (many 4–5 ★ reviews) while recording quality receives both positive and negative remarks, resulting in a weighted average that lands just under 4 ★.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Second‑set levels are clear (Phil bass, Brent keyboards).  Overall decent mix but noticeable noise‑reduction artifacts and occasional low‑end distortion; only the second set appears to have been recorded.

standout_songs: China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
show_quality.setlist_flow: The setlist flows nicely, with a strong opening trio, Althea early on, an upbeat China > Rider transition to open the second set, and a seamless Playin’/Space/Playin’ Reprise into Morning Dew that closes the night.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Great jamming with Brent during the Playin' sequence; guitar work highlighted as energetic.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass is clearer than on other Aud recordings, especially at the start of set 2.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent's keyboards shine in Space and the Playin' sandwich, adding texture to the second‑set jams.
band_member_comments.Drums: Furious drum work noted, driving the intensity of the performance.
